title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s affecting multiple people definitely sounds like something changed on the backend side rather than a permissions issue on your end.

To start narrowing this down: **were there any deployments, infrastructure changes, or updates to your authentication/SSO provider around the time this started a couple days ago?** Also, when you hit the 403, is it always on the initial dashboard load, or does it sometimes happen mid-session (e.g., you're using the dashboard fine and then a specific action returns 403)?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
I'm not sure about any backend deployments — I'm not really in the loop on that stuff. I do know that I was recently given a new 'analyst' role, that was around the same time this started, maybe two days ago? Not sure if that's related.

As for when the 403 happens, it's kind of both. Sometimes the dashboard won't load at all, other times I'll be clicking around and a page will just come back 403. It feels pretty random honestly — I've been assuming it's some kind of network issue on our end.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That new 'analyst' role is a really important detail — the timing lines up perfectly with when this started, so let's pull on that thread.

When you hit a 403 while clicking around the dashboard, can you pay attention to **which specific pages or actions** trigger it? For example, does viewing tasks always work but editing them returns 403? Or does a specific section of the dashboard (like reports, settings, admin areas) consistently fail while others consistently load?

Basically, I'm trying to figure out whether the 403s are truly random or whether they're tied to specific features that the 'analyst' role might not have permissions for. If you could try a few actions and note which ones succeed vs. fail, that would really help narrow this down.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I tried paying attention to it today and honestly it doesn't seem to be tied to any specific page or action. I got a 403 just loading the main dashboard, then I refreshed and it worked fine. Then I clicked into a report and that worked, then tried opening another report and got a 403 again. It really does seem random — like any page can fail. If I just refresh when it happens, it usually works on the second or third try. I'd say it fails maybe a third of the time roughly? It's not like reports fail but tasks work, it's more like everything fails sometimes.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the fact that it's truly random across all pages and a refresh fixes it tells me this probably isn't a simple 'the analyst role lacks permission X' situation (if it were, specific pages would *always* fail).

Here's what I need to narrow down next: **you mentioned other people on your team are seeing this too — do you know if they also received the 'analyst' role around the same time you did?** Or are people with different roles (or unchanged roles) also hitting these intermittent 403s?

This will tell us whether the role change is actually connected or just a coincidence in timing.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, I think the people on my team who are having the same issue were also switched to the analyst role around the same time I was. My manager still has admin access and she said she hasn't seen any 403s at all. So yeah, it might actually be connected to the analyst role somehow — but it's weird because it's not consistent, you know? If the role was broken I'd expect it to fail every time, not just sometimes.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with 'analyst' role — likely authorization caching or multi-instance permission inconsistency","problem":"Users who were recently assigned the 'analyst' role are experiencing intermittent 403 Forbidden errors across all dashboard pages. Approximately one-third of page loads fail, but retrying (refreshing) typically succeeds within 2-3 attempts. Users with the 'admin' role are completely unaffected. The issue began approximately two days ago, coinciding with the analyst role being assigned to the affected users.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The 'analyst' role's permissions are inconsistently resolved across requests, most likely due to one of: (1) Multiple application server instances behind a load balancer where one or more instances have stale or misconfigured permission/role mappings for the analyst role — the ~33% failure rate suggests roughly 1-in-3 backends may be affected. (2) An authorization caching layer (e.g., Redis, in-memory cache) with a race condition or partial invalidation issue introduced when the analyst role was created or modified. (3) The analyst role definition itself may reference permissions that intermittently fail to resolve (e.g., a wildcard or dynamic permission that depends on an unreliable lookup).","reproduction_steps":["Assign a user the 'analyst' role in TaskFlow","Log in as that user and navigate to the dashboard","Click through various pages (reports, tasks, any section)","Observe that approximately 1 in 3 page loads returns a 403 Forbidden","Refresh the page — it will typically load successfully on retry","Repeat with an 'admin' role user to confirm they are NOT affected"],"environment":"Production TaskFlow instance. Affects multiple users across at least one team. All affected users share the 'analyst' role assigned approximately 2 days ago. Admin-role users are unaffected. No other environment specifics available from reporter.","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the 'analyst' role are affected. Roughly one-third of page loads fail, degrading productivity significantly. Users can work around it by refreshing, but this causes frustration and lost time. If the analyst role is being rolled out to more users, impact will grow.","recommended_fix":"1. Check whether the application runs behind a load balancer with multiple backend instances. If so, compare the analyst role's permission configuration across all instances — look for stale deployments or inconsistent permission caches. 2. Inspect the authorization caching layer: check cache TTLs, invalidation logic, and whether the analyst role's permissions were fully propagated when the role was created. 3. Review the analyst role definition for any permissions that depend on dynamic lookups or external services that could intermittently fail. 4. Check application logs filtered to 403 responses for analyst-role users — correlate with specific server instance IDs to confirm whether failures cluster on specific backends. 5. As a quick mitigation, verify that restarting app servers or flushing the auth cache resolves the issue.","proposed_test_case":"Create an integration test that: (1) assigns the 'analyst' role to a test user, (2) makes 50+ sequential authenticated requests to various dashboard endpoints, and (3) asserts that zero requests return 403. Run this test against each backend instance individually (bypassing the load balancer) to identify inconsistent nodes. Additionally, add a regression test that verifies new role assignments are fully propagated across all authorization backends before returning success.","information_gaps":["Number of application server instances and load balancer configuration (reporter does not have backend visibility)","Whether any deployments or infrastructure changes occurred around the same timeframe (reporter is not in the loop on deployments)","Exact authorization mechanism used (RBAC framework, caching layer, session store)","Server-side logs showing which instances serve the 403 responses"]}
